reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance system design, reporting practice, and measurement frameworks
reassignments.rationale: Perspective focuses on missing gender-identity fields as a reporting and measurement-framework problem in COVID-19 trial data rather than on burden estimation.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance data validation and completeness assessment
reassignments.rationale: Assesses discontinuation and missing result reporting in a trial registry, centering completeness and reporting-quality evaluation.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Evidence syntheses on burden, surveillance, and reporting gaps
reassignments.rationale: Systematic review synthesizes protocol and publication reporting gaps for pregnancy outcomes in epidemic vaccine trials.
Longitudinal representative serocohort directly measures infection prevalence, vaccination-related antibodies, and underreporting in the community.
Uses wastewater surveillance and case:wastewater ratios as a complementary population surveillance stream for hidden COVID-19 burden.
Evaluates county wastewater metrics as an alternative surveillance signal for cases and hospitalizations under home-test underascertainment.
Perspective centers wastewater surveillance as the complementary data source to be integrated with models for tracking and prediction.
Bayesian epidemic forecasting model explicitly incorporates missing and delayed case reports to improve long-range predictions.
Validates EHR-derived ICD-based chronic HCV case definitions against chart review for surveillance use.
Bayesian hierarchical model estimates reporting probabilities and corrected gestational-syphilis incidence across microregions.
Uses wastewater-based epidemiology to detect community drug-resistant TB signals beyond conventional clinical reporting.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance system design and reporting interventions
reassignments.rationale: Implements and evaluates a knowledge-rule alert system to reduce missed infectious-disease reporting by clinicians.
Scoping review synthesizes digital-tool approaches for post-discharge SSI surveillance.
Evaluates a web-based mail-order self-testing platform as a non-clinic data collection and detection pathway for STI surveillance.
Compartmental model is used specifically to infer ascertainment rates and other epidemic parameters from reported African COVID-19 counts.
Links hospitalization codes to test results to quantify RSV coding misclassification and underreporting in registry data.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Self-report and behavioral measurement bias assessment
reassignments.rationale: Compares self-reported condomless sex with biomarker evidence to measure behavioral underreporting.
Randomized evaluation of SMS/CATI active AEFI detection versus passive vaccine safety reporting.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance system improvement and reporting-enhancement strategies
reassignments.rationale: Assesses how free sampling, screening panels, and program changes improve bovine abortion submission and surveillance performance.
Cost-of-illness analysis depends on pertussis incidence and underreporting adjustments to estimate true economic burden.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Self-report and behavioral measurement bias assessment
reassignments.rationale: Uses a biomarker subsample to assess underreporting of maternal alcohol exposure relative to self-report.
Review/report is organized around meningococcal case-definition, diagnostic, and surveillance gaps across the region.
Prospective cohort with repeat serology directly estimates SARS-CoV-2 incidence and risk factors beyond routine diagnosed-case surveillance.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Counterfactual policy and vaccination impact models
reassignments.rationale: Constructs pre-vaccination counterfactual incidence to estimate reported vaccine-preventable cases averted.
Systematic review and exploratory meta-analysis of vaccine efficacy studies is an evidence-synthesis design.
Evaluates training and mentoring to improve adverse-event reporting within a pharmacovigilance system.
SEIR-type model with vaccine immunity and explicit under-reporting studies outbreak dynamics after policy relaxation.
Forecasting comparison incorporates weather drivers and estimated detection probability to project dengue epidemic waves.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance data validation and completeness assessment
reassignments.rationale: Compares written rabies-exposure reports with two electronic reporting systems to identify reporting gaps and incompleteness.
Bayesian conditional autoregressive spatiotemporal model estimates meteorological associations in geographically structured COVID-19 case data.
Uses spontaneous EudraVigilance reports and disproportionality analyses for antibiotic-associated CDI safety surveillance.
Systematic review of post-vaccination case reports synthesizes unsolicited adverse-event safety evidence.
Narrative review centers the structure, performance, and challenges of COVID-19 vaccine pharmacovigilance.
Reconstructs hidden first-wave infections from hospital admissions via inverse-problem deconvolution.
Methodological paper uses pairwise/network epidemic models to infer disease and network parameters under possible underreporting.
Linked-record cohort quantifies surgical-site infection incidence missed by routine surveillance after discharge.
Inverse modeling is used to reconstruct time-varying SARS-CoV-2 reporting rates from observed incidence, deaths, and vaccination data.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Counterfactual policy and vaccination impact models
reassignments.rationale: Bayesian semi-mechanistic counterfactual analysis permutes policy trajectories across countries using reported and estimated unreported deaths.
Pilot wastewater and non-sewered sanitation surveillance system for cholera and typhoid extends surveillance in low-clinical-testing settings.
Develops a formal framework for how under-reporting and delays degrade inference from observed epidemic curves.
Stochastic scaling model estimates true illnesses, hospitalizations, and deaths after correcting Legionnaires surveillance undercapture.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance system evaluation and reporting-quality studies
reassignments.rationale: Evaluates operational contact-tracing coverage and determinants of missing contacts within a surveillance system.
Shows how a two-step CDI testing algorithm yields surveillance undercount because NAAT+/toxin- clinically relevant cases are excluded.
Uses a power-law model to estimate the number of foodborne outbreaks missed by detection and reporting systems.
Uses local Moran's I to identify anthrax incidence clusters and spatial targeting priorities without fitting endemic-epidemic transmission models.
Bayesian hierarchical inference jointly models true infections, delayed reports, and time-varying underreporting across multiple outcomes.
Excess-death extrapolation from obituary data estimates mortality far above officially reported COVID-19 deaths.
Demonstrates a combined PCR/serology method that substantially improves re-infection detection over testing alone.
Uses molecular detection and sequencing to document circulation of an underdiagnosed arbovirus for surveillance purposes.
Deterministic transmission models with vectors, hosts, seasonality, and reporting processes are fit to explain Ross River virus dynamics.
Household survey directly quantifies immunization-error prevalence and compares it with state reporting records to measure underreporting.
Review synthesizes diagnostic advances needed to reduce rabies underconfirmation and surveillance underreporting.
Population rapid-test survey estimates asymptomatic infection and household underreporting among school children.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance system design and reporting interventions
reassignments.rationale: Project overview evaluates multifaceted interventions that markedly increased infectious-disease notification rates.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance under-ascertainment, burden-estimation, and reporting-completeness designs
reassignments.rationale: Viewpoint is centered on proposed pulmonary long-COVID phenotyping and notes prevalence under-ascertainment, but does not fit a narrower allowed empirical concept.
Modified ARIMA model forecasts weekly dengue incidence from surveillance time series.
Uses Google Trends search volume as an auxiliary near-real-time signal to improve surveillance-model performance.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Self-controlled case-series models with exposure-timing error
reassignments.rationale: Modified self-controlled case series on VAERS estimates post-vaccination myocarditis risk while addressing passive-reporting distortions.
Cross-sectional population testing quantifies attack rates, underdetection, and notification after community transmission begins.
Additional serologic testing among dengue-suspected patients identifies overlapping hepatitis infections missed by routine case classification.
Narrative review and expert recommendations focus on adult pertussis evidence and surveillance gaps caused by underrecognition and underreporting.
Review highlights lack of a standard TDR infection definition and resulting underreporting in the literature.
Serial serology and variant sequencing are used as an ongoing population monitoring platform for hidden infections and immunity.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Interrupted time series and segmented regression
reassignments.rationale: Mixed-methods study includes interrupted time-series analysis of health-service utilization around COVID-19 disruptions.
Planned analysis of a cluster-randomized trial estimates population impact of universal testing/treatment plus TB screening.
Retrospective case finding compares diagnosed visceral-leishmaniasis cases with notified cases to quantify underreporting.
Systematic review with random-effects meta-analysis of childhood TB outcomes is an evidence-synthesis study.
Derives pregnancy-specific renal reference intervals to avoid misclassification and underdetection of abnormal renal function.
Representative survey with PCR, serology, questionnaire, and lineage detection estimates active infection, prevalence, and hidden burden.
Simulation study quantifies bias in retrospective-cohort vaccine-effectiveness estimators under varying underreporting assumptions.
Bayesian state-level spatiotemporal mapping explicitly predicts infection risk and reporting-rate heterogeneity across place and time.
Statewide cluster-randomized serosurveillance with linked mosquito testing monitors dengue transmission during COVID-era reporting disruptions.
SIRDS model with fuzzy epidemic-period transitions estimates time-varying parameters and underreporting across waves.
Years-of-life-lost and excess-mortality study estimates COVID mortality burden where official death reporting is heterogeneous and incomplete.
Narrative review synthesizes vaccine pharmacovigilance strategies, legislation, and underreporting challenges.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance system design, reporting practice, and measurement frameworks
reassignments.rationale: Commentary examines selective microbiology result reporting as a reporting-practice design problem with underreporting tradeoffs.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Spatial risk mapping with detection/reporting adjustment
reassignments.rationale: Bayesian spatiotemporal occupancy model estimates latent endemicity while explicitly correcting for imperfect case detection.
Expert-panel review focuses on adult pertussis detection, diagnosis, case-definition, and reporting gaps.
Method paper modifies the SIR framework to estimate the reporting fraction from partially observed epidemic data.
Review plus outbreak/genomic analysis is aimed at understanding regional chikungunya transmission dynamics under surveillance gaps.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance system evaluation and reporting-quality studies
reassignments.rationale: Describes and evaluates a national biovigilance program, including persistent donor-derived infection underreporting.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Self-report and behavioral measurement bias assessment
reassignments.rationale: Quantifies recall decay in reported contacts during contact-tracing interviews as a human reporting-bias process.
Biweekly household symptom/swab surveillance with serial serology directly measures COVID incidence in an urban population.
Google Trends is evaluated as an auxiliary digital signal aligned with TB notification data before and after mandatory reporting.
Bayesian transmission framework infers true infections and daily reporting ratios from death data and age structure.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance system design and reporting-improvement studies
reassignments.rationale: Pilot develops and tests modified VAP surveillance definitions tailored to low-resource hospital settings.
Nationwide residual-sera surveillance characterizes infection- and vaccine-derived antibody prevalence after substantial case underreporting.
Systematic review plus official-report synthesis assesses zoonotic-TB occurrence and surveillance underreporting across regions.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Risk-factor and intervention studies with reporting-bias implications
reassignments.rationale: Statewide linkage study evaluates infection exposure as a colorectal-cancer risk factor while acknowledging surveillance underreporting as a bias source.
Cross-sectional serologic outbreak investigation explicitly estimates the proportion and predictors of unreported COVID-19 cases.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance data validation and completeness assessment
reassignments.rationale: Compares two cattle antibiotic-use reporting registries to identify systematic underreporting and agreement patterns.
Systematic review and meta-analysis summarizes waterborne parasite prevalence and discusses underestimation in outbreak reporting.
Observational analysis describes secondary transmission patterns from surveillance data without fitting a formal transmission model.
Six-compartment optimal-control model embeds reported and unreported symptomatic classes to study intervention effects.
SMS-based cohort-event monitoring is an active digital pharmacovigilance design for pediatric AEFI detection.
Serial serosurveys estimate case-detection ratios and validate surveillance reliability in island populations.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Incidence, prevalence, and burden estimation from surveillance or survey data
reassignments.rationale: Seroprevalence study estimates hidden exposure burden of louping ill virus in dogs.
Systematic review highlights outbreak trends together with underreporting and diagnostic gaps for Chandipura virus.
Integrates wastewater and river-water SARS-CoV-2 measurements with epidemiologic data to reveal underreported transmission.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance system design, reporting practice, and measurement frameworks
reassignments.rationale: Methodological overview catalogs data sources and interpretation limits for studying fungal-disease disparities under underdiagnosis and underreporting.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Counterfactual policy and vaccination impact models
reassignments.rationale: Counterfactual vaccination-impact model estimates deaths averted after adjusting reported COVID mortality upward.
Rural household mortality survey estimates excess pandemic mortality where official COVID death reporting is incomplete.
Tests suspected dengue cases for leptospirosis to identify underdiagnosis from overlapping febrile case definitions.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance system evaluation and reporting improvement
reassignments.rationale: Mixed-methods assessment evaluates feasibility, sensitivity, costs, and sustainability barriers of community-based surveillance.
Fits a reduced-form SIR model to Omicron incidence and studies parameter identifiability in epidemic dynamics.
Statewide cluster-randomized serologic and vector-surveillance study tracks dengue signals during COVID-era reporting disruptions.
Regional invasive-pneumococcal surveillance analysis describes notification trends, serotypes, and likely underestimation of cases.
Pilot combines community reporting with field diagnostics to improve rabies case detection and reduce underreporting.
Systematic review and proportional meta-analysis estimates HBV reactivation incidence while explicitly handling sparse and zero-event studies.
Survey-based assessment documents hidden COVID burden, vaccination uptake, and registration barriers in an indigenous population.
Develops a selective-pressure metric from variant-frequency dynamics to provide early epidemic-growth signal under case underreporting.
Review is centered on TBE surveillance shortcomings, laboratory-access gaps, and resulting underreporting.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Incidence, prevalence, and burden estimation from surveillance or survey data
reassignments.rationale: Human seroprevalence component is used to reveal pertussis circulation hidden from routine reporting.
Bayesian symbolic regression learns closed-form models to estimate real-time incidence from reported cases and testing rates.
National serosurvey is analyzed with Bayesian geostatistical mapping to estimate HEV spatial seroprevalence.
Outbreak report primarily documents epidemiologic and laboratory features of an underreported foodborne pathogen.
Cause-of-death decomposition interprets life-expectancy losses while explicitly considering underreported COVID-related mortality.
Systematic review synthesizes myocarditis incidence and prognosis evidence in athletes after infection or vaccination.
Nationwide population-based serosurvey estimates infection-induced antibodies and the infection underreporting factor.
Scoping review synthesizes how COVID disrupted malaria surveillance, case management, and reporting across Asia.
Random-forest models forecast environmental infection risk from surveillance and environmental covariates.
Molecular and phylogenetic characterization of measles-confirmed cases is a genomic surveillance study.
Clinician guide synthesizes pertussis epidemiology, diagnostic limitations, and underreporting barriers relevant to surveillance.
Method paper develops nonparametric pseudo-likelihood estimation for semi-competing risks with event misascertainment.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance system design, reporting practice, and measurement frameworks
reassignments.rationale: Recommendations paper evaluates diagnostic-system gaps and proposes point-of-care testing as a framework for better respiratory surveillance.
Evaluates the diagnostic yield of TB contact investigation as an active case-finding strategy and quantifies missed cases.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Counterfactual policy and vaccination impact models
reassignments.rationale: Age-structured model compares vaccination prioritization strategies while accounting for age-specific under-reporting.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance system evaluation and reporting improvement
reassignments.rationale: Mixed-methods outbreak assessment identifies rabies reporting failures, stockouts, and One Health communication gaps.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance system design, reporting practice, and measurement frameworks
reassignments.rationale: Document analysis tracks legislative evolution of HAI definitions, reporting structures, and barriers to accurate reporting.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Interrupted time series and segmented regression
reassignments.rationale: Interrupted time-series analysis estimates the reporting shock to HIV/AIDS case counts at the start of COVID NPIs.
Systematic review is organized around disease distribution evidence and cross-country underreporting gaps in aquaculture surveillance.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Counterfactual policy and vaccination impact models
reassignments.rationale: Bayesian SEIR counterfactual estimates deaths prevented by Sweden's vaccination program.
Wastewater surveillance is explicitly evaluated as a less socioeconomically biased complement to laboratory-based case surveillance.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Interrupted time series and segmented regression
reassignments.rationale: Time-series natural experiment quantifies HIV/AIDS case underreporting in Peru during the COVID disruption period.
Machine-learning geospatial models predict TBEV-suitable areas and identify potential risk zones beyond observed reports.
Global review emphasizes uneven diagnostics and underreporting as barriers to understanding APPeV distribution.
National burden estimates are produced by adjusting surveillance counts for underreporting and underdiagnosis.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance data validation and completeness assessment
reassignments.rationale: Compares molecularly identified Q-fever cases against national reporting channels to quantify underreporting.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance data validation and completeness assessment
reassignments.rationale: Data linkage sensitivity analysis measures how much hepatitis mortality is missed by death-registration-based reporting alone.
Systematic review is about sampling, reporting, and stigma barriers that obstruct accurate HIV genetic surveillance in MENA.
Agreement study validates parent-reported HPV vaccination status against daughter report and characterizes under-reporting patterns.
Cross-sectional serosurvey measures cumulative SARS-CoV-2 exposure in a settlement where routine testing was limited.
Review argues for national foodborne-disease burden estimation and stronger surveillance to address underdiagnosis and underreporting.
Disproportionality-based evaluation of South Africa's AEFI reporting system is a vaccine pharmacovigilance study.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Spatial risk mapping with detection/reporting adjustment
reassignments.rationale: Ecological niche model explicitly aims to map FMD risk while accounting for underreported outbreaks.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance ascertainment, validation, and correction studies
reassignments.rationale: Standardization-based adjustment corrects reported SARS-CoV-2 cases for differential testing by age and sex.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Spatial models of hidden burden and cryptic spread
reassignments.rationale: Bayesian community-level hotspot model combines notifications and diagnostic data to estimate undiagnosed TB burden spatially.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Spatial risk mapping with detection/reporting adjustment
reassignments.rationale: Paired occurrence and reporting ecological-niche models identify Kazakhstani HPAI areas with high likely underreporting.
Systematic review and meta-analysis evaluate AI-based HAI surveillance systems and their readiness for real practice.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Surveillance system evaluation and reporting-quality studies
reassignments.rationale: Compares passive and active HPAI surveillance components to assess their relative contribution to outbreak detection and reporting.
